items.title: What is Overfitting in Trading? - AlgoTrading101 Blog
items.author: algotrading101.com
items.snippet: The r/algotrading community consistently highlights the gap between backtest performance and live trading results as the central challenge for retail algo traders. Recurring themes include overfitting (curve fitting) to historical data — a 2014 study cited across the community found 44% of published trading strategies failed to replicate results on new data. Community members frequently share backtests showing inflated returns (some claiming 3000%+), while experienced members caution these are a

items.title: Microservices Based Algorithmic Trading System - Medium
items.author: usquant349.medium.com
items.snippet: Automated trading system architecture discussions across r/algotrading and r/quant consistently converge on a modular, event-driven design with four core layers: market data ingestion, signal generation, risk management, and order execution. The dominant pattern in 2025-2026 is decoupling these components via an event bus (e.g., Redis pub/sub or Kafka), so each subsystem reacts to market events asynchronously without tight coupling.
